content:
Currently, Rust for Linux doesn’t have a rust_expand command, so if I want to see the expanded
Rust code, it’s a bit of a hassle — I have to run rustc manually with all the right flags.

For example, if I want to see the expanded Rust code for rust/kernel, I have to run this long command:

OBJTREE=/home/jckeep/C/linux rustc +nightly --edition=2021 -Zunpretty=expanded rust/kernel/lib.rs \
    -Cpanic=abort -Cembed-bitcode=n -Clto=n -Cforce-unwind-tables=n -Ccodegen-units=1 \
    -Csymbol-mangling-version=v0 -Crelocation-model=static -Zfunction-sections=n \
    -Ctarget-feature="-neon" -Cforce-unwind-tables=n -Zbranch-protection=bti,pac-ret \
    -Copt-level=2 -Cdebug-assertions=y -Coverflow-checks=y -Cforce-frame-pointers=y -Cdebuginfo=1 \
    --crate-name kernel --extern ffi --extern build_error --extern macros --extern bindings --extern uapi \
    -L./rust --target aarch64-unknown-none @./include/generated/rustc_cfg  --crate-type=rlib

That’s way too much. Maybe we could add a simple command to expand the kernel’s Rust code more easily. :thinking:
I have used rust-analyzer to expand macros in kernel Rust code once, maybe that could work for you? Not sure what you're trying to expand.

Lukas Fischer said:

I have used rust-analyzer to expand macros in kernel Rust code once, maybe that could work for you? Not sure what you're trying to expand.

yeah, how to use rust-analyzer to expand macros, I didn't know it. :rolling_on_the_floor_laughing:

I just checked, since I wasn't sure anymore. I use vscode, in Rust code when I place the cursor in a macro call it shows me a tooltip/light bulb with the "inline macro" option. I just realized that this does not work with derive macros, but it does work with proc macros (for example module!).
there are <path/to/rust/file>.rsi targets, so for example make samples/rust/rust_minimal.rsi will create that file and write the expanded code into that.
make rust/lib/kernel.rsi fails with several weird errors, so there is probably something wrong in the makefile there
It is not wrong, it is simply not supported for the crates there yet.
I see, depending on how intertwined the code is, you can just copy it into one of the samples and just expand it there

Benno Lossin发言道：

there are <path/to/rust/file>.rsi targets, so for example make samples/rust/rust_minimal.rsi will create that file and write the expanded code into that.

:grinning_face_with_smiling_eyes:it’s nice

at some point I thought about renaming the extension to .expanded.rs instead of .rsi, since that makes it an .rs file and makes it much easier to remember (every time I use this feature, I have to look up its name)
